mechanism_rationale: Direct human PVR/PDR membrane evidence and experimental ocular blockade evidence.
key_boundary: PDR-biased expression; efficacy still requires intervention testing.

mechanism_rationale: Linked to pericyte-myofibroblast transformation in PDR and promotes fibrosis in other organs.
key_boundary: PDR-biased and lacks direct ocular intervention evidence.

mechanism_rationale: Marks pathogenic myofibroblasts and promotes TGF-beta-linked fibrosis; ocular intervention evidence is not yet direct.
key_boundary: PDR-biased and endothelial expression requires safety review.

experimental_role: Broad ECM positive control; safety-limited target
mechanism_rationale: Central injury-associated ECM and potentially targetable through disease-associated fibronectin domains; broad normal-matrix expression is a risk.
key_boundary: Broad ECM and non-target expression limits specificity.

mechanism_rationale: Extracellular activator of TLR4-FAK/TGF-beta fibrosis; retinal fibrosis association but no direct PVR/PDR blockade study.
key_boundary: Fails CPM >= 2 in all patients and is strongly PDR-biased.

mechanism_rationale: Fibrosis-associated secreted factor with emerging intervention evidence outside the eye.
Emerging extracellular regulator that can promote TGF-beta/SMAD fibrosis; ocular mechanism is unvalidated.
key_boundary: Cross-disease balanced but ocular mechanism remains unvalidated.

mechanism_rationale: Present in PVR/PDR membranes and targetable, but PAI-1 can be pro- or anti-fibrotic depending on context.
ECM-remodeling marker with context-dependent effects; inhibition could increase matrix degradation or injury unpredictably.
Complement inhibitor with retinal protective functions; expression support must not be interpreted as rationale for inhibition.
key_boundary: Retinal-protective factor; do not infer inhibition benefit.
